title: Qubit Platform Comparison
description: Compare the leading qubit platforms against one application goal and argue which hardware approach is most credible for that use case.
whyItMatters: The field is full of platform claims. This project teaches how to compare coherence, control, scaling, and operating constraints without collapsing into hype.

steps.title: Choose the target application
steps.description: Pick a use case such as chemistry simulation, networking, or precision sensing.
steps.tips: A specific application produces a better comparison than a generic one.

steps.title: Select the candidate platforms
steps.description: Compare at least three platforms such as superconducting, trapped-ion, and photonic systems.
steps.tips: Use common comparison axes so the trade stays fair.

steps.title: Score the engineering trade-offs
steps.description: Assess coherence, gate quality, scaling path, infrastructure burden, and likely roadmap risk.
steps.tips: Explain why each criterion matters for the chosen application.

steps.title: Write a recommendation
steps.description: Name the most credible platform and the strongest reason you might still choose a different one.

steps.title: Reflect on hype versus evidence
steps.description: Identify one claim that sounds impressive but weakens under closer engineering scrutiny.
steps.tips: This is a judgement exercise, not just a spreadsheet exercise.

deliverables: Short note on one overhyped or underappreciated trade-off

rubric.description: The platforms are judged on relevant, application-linked criteria.
The recommendation is evidence-based rather than buzzword-based.
The trade study is concise and reviewable.
